nodes.label: AI as proofreading assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as full writer or replacement
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to proofread emails before I send them

nodes.label: AI as mentor for learning
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch that degrades skills
nodes.grounding_span: I think using AI has even improved my writing - I've learned from it

nodes.label: AI for efficient document comparison
nodes.grounding_span: I uploaded two PDFs and asked AI to find the differences

nodes.label: Preserving personal enjoyment and skills in writing
nodes.grounding_span: I still want the reviews to sound like me - I never have the AI write the reviews

nodes.label: AI as collaborator for iterative refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I'll go back and forth a few times with the AI to get an end result I feel is perfect

nodes.label: Selective AI use based on task importance
nodes.label_negative: Over-reliance on AI for trivial tasks
nodes.grounding_span: there's no need to do that for a 'Confirming receipt, thanks!' email

nodes.label: Effective professional communication
nodes.grounding_span: I want to ensure I didn't make any grammatical errors or spelling mistakes

nodes.label: Personal competence and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I've learned from it rather than just copied and pasted its suggestions

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: anything that's changed is immediately flagged to me

nodes.label: Creative authenticity and human expression
nodes.grounding_span: I still want the reviews to sound like me

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: The AI ended up identifying words that differed in PDF 1, but those words didn't exist

nodes.label: Reluctance to use AI for writing
nodes.grounding_span: I think using AI has even improved my writing

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for document analysis
nodes.grounding_span: This was frustrating and I ended up having to read both PDFs word for word

nodes.label: Preference for human writing
nodes.grounding_span: I actually enjoy writing, and taking this first pass by myself also helps me learn

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: I think I will start using it more once I'm better aware of its capabilities

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with limited back-and-forth
nodes.grounding_span: I'll go back and forth a few times with the AI to get an end result I feel is perfect

nodes.label: Domain-specific calibration of AI use
nodes.grounding_span: there's no need to do that for a 'Confirming receipt, thanks!' email
